sourceMovie.title: Spy Kids
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Spy Kids (2001) as a family theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The family and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Phineas and Ferb The Movie: Across the 2nd Dimension
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Spy Kids clicked for you, Phineas and Ferb The Movie: Across the 2nd Dimension (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in family and action, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Spy Kids?
faq.answer: Try Phineas and Ferb The Movie: Across the 2nd Dimension, Secret Headquarters, Big Hero 6—each shares DNA with Spy Kids while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Spy Kids?
faq.answer: Spy Kids (2001) is best described as Family, Action, Comedy, Adventure, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Spy Kids part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Spy Kids Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
